source: Business → Business (B2B) Scenario Pack (illustrative, non-exhaustive · not legal advice · counsel review required) · Mal-CRAM-US-01 v2.3 (CRAM-US-2026-07-FREEZE-03) · MAL-TA-01 v1.0 · MSB Compliance Risk Typology Register (Draft v0.1) · MAL-TM-PPC-CATALOG v1.0 (B2B, 20 purpose codes)
note: A Global-Accounts business ENTITY (established in one of the nine permitted corridor jurisdictions, holding a US account) pays ANOTHER business (B2B). Both parties are entities: the payer is the onboarded customer (full KYB, UBO, §6.4 prohibited-type screen); the receiving business is a screened counterparty checked for a related-party link and — for trade flows — reconciled against goods/values/shipping. B2B is the perimeter's highest TBML surface: over/under-invoicing, circular/round-trip trade, related-party invoicing, intercompany sweeps, dividend/royalty/capital round-tripping. Establishment is the gate; both structures are screened; the trade/relationship documents decide. Guidance only; the customer rating is set by the CRAM composite.

scenarios.title: Pakistani trading LLC — import payment to an unrelated foreign supplier (workable, High/EDD)
scenarios.profile: General-trading/export LLC in Pakistan paying a foreign supplier for imported goods with shipping evidence. LLC · 2 UBOs resident PK, each >25%, no nominees; ownership resolves to natural persons; UBO certification held.
scenarios.geography: Establishment PK (permitted, passes G0) · UBOs PK · SoW = business profits · SoF = trade receipts
scenarios.flow: B2B-02 payment for goods (import) — receiving business is an unrelated licensed foreign supplier; related-party test: payer & supplier NOT under common control; supplier existence/licence verified
scenarios.corridor: US→PK / import corridor High; TBML scenario 5.15
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO clear; supplier screened (sanctions/adverse media) and confirmed unrelated & licensed
scenarios.documents: KYB (incorporation, active trade licence, registry extract, shareholder register, UBO ID+PoA, certification). Additional: commercial invoice + bill of lading + customs declaration + contract for reconciliation; dual-use goods screening; supplier KYB; confirm no 3rd-party (non-invoice) payer
scenarios.expectedActivity: Import payments reconciling to shipping/customs and business scale; consistent supplier set
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Over/under-invoicing; phantom shipments; unit-price outliers; payment to a third country unrelated to trade; 3rd-party (non-invoice) payers
scenarios.sarPosture: Over/under-invoicing or no lawful purpose → decline + SAR (TBML)
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, High/EDD if documents reconcile. Over/under-invoicing or no lawful purpose → decline + SAR (TBML)

scenarios.title: Pakistani 'consultancy' LLC — vague management/consulting fees to a secrecy-jurisdiction company (shell-payment)
scenarios.profile: Services LLC paying large 'consulting/management fees' to a company in a secrecy jurisdiction with no deliverable footprint. LLC · single UBO · thin substance.
scenarios.geography: Establishment PK · UBO PK · SoW/SoF opaque
scenarios.flow: B2B-04 professional/consulting services — counterparty in a low-transparency jurisdiction; related-party test: possible hidden common control, counterparty opacity high
scenarios.corridor: US→PK High; TYP-PK-003 fake-service; shell-activity red flags
scenarios.screening: Payer clear on lists; counterparty adverse-media/shell markers; secrecy-jurisdiction flag
scenarios.documents: Engagement letter + deliverable description; counterparty existence/KYB; consulting-fee share of outflows; substance evidence both sides
scenarios.expectedActivity: Consulting fees tied to documented deliverables to a genuine counterparty
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Vague 'management fees'; no deliverable; counterparty in a secrecy jurisdiction; consulting-fee share of outflows dominant
scenarios.sarPosture: No substance / shell-payment label → SAR; decline the flow
scenarios.outcome: EDD; decline + SAR on no-substance

scenarios.title: Pakistani entity with a nominee director & opaque offshore holding — unresolvable UBO (refuse)
scenarios.profile: LLC whose registered director is a nominee; ownership runs through an opaque offshore holding; UBO not disclosed. Wants to run B2B flows. LLC · nominee director · offshore holding layer · UBO NOT identifiable.
scenarios.geography: Establishment PK · UBO unknown
scenarios.flow: Any B2B payment; related-party test cannot be assessed without UBO
scenarios.corridor: US→PK High; structure fails before corridor scoring
scenarios.screening: Cannot complete — UBO not identifiable; nominee/front markers
scenarios.rating: Refuse (Priority-1) — a hard refusal, not a High rating
scenarios.documents: AI first requests the full ownership chart to natural persons + nominee-resolution evidence; unresolved within the RFI window → refuse
scenarios.expectedActivity: N/A
scenarios.behaviourWatch: N/A at onboarding. Existing customer where a nominee/front later surfaces → freeze + exit
scenarios.sarPosture: Nominee-front / structural opacity — refuse; SAR if surfaced on an existing relationship
scenarios.outcome: Refuse on unresolved UBO (§6.2, Priority-1)

scenarios.title: Pakistani LLC with a Lebanese UBO — restricted-country UBO (Rule 1), paying an unrelated supplier
scenarios.profile: PK-established trading LLC; controlling UBO is a Lebanese national whose SoW originates in Lebanon; pays an unrelated licensed supplier for goods. LLC · controlling UBO Lebanese (restricted, non-OFAC) · other UBO PK · resolves to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment PK (permitted) · UBO nationality LB · UBO SoW = Lebanon · entity SoF = PK trade receipts
scenarios.flow: B2B-01/02 goods payment to an unrelated licensed supplier; no Lebanon counterparty/corridor; related-party test: payer & supplier unrelated, supplier verified
scenarios.corridor: US→PK High + restricted-country-UBO screening; Lebanon counterparty/corridor blocked
scenarios.screening: Lebanese UBO → screening escalation + Rule 1 on Lebanon-origin SoW; entity/other nodes/counterparty clear
scenarios.rating: High floor (restricted-country UBO SoW, Rule 1) — documented UBO SoW/SoF, nexus screening, senior approval
scenarios.documents: Full KYB; Lebanese UBO SoW (company licence/financials/dividends) + how proceeds left Lebanon (remittance receipts) + nexus screening + senior-approval record; trade docs for the goods leg; confirm no Lebanon counterparty
scenarios.expectedActivity: Goods payments to unrelated verified suppliers; UBO capital evidenced
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any Lebanon counterparty/corridor (→ reject); unexplained UBO top-ups from Lebanon; trade-doc mismatch
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, High-floor EDD, senior sign-off; Lebanon counterparties disabled. Unevidenced UBO SoW → hold then decline

scenarios.title: Egyptian import/export company — invoicing anomalies with a foreign counterparty (TBML core case)
scenarios.profile: EG trading company paying/receiving for goods where declared goods/values don't reconcile with shipping docs (over-invoicing red flag). LLC/JSC · shareholders resolve to natural persons · UBO certification held.
scenarios.geography: Establishment EG (grey-list) · UBOs EG · SoW/SoF = business income
scenarios.flow: B2B-02 import / B2B-03 export proceeds — foreign counterparty; related-party check on the counterparty; watch circular trade
scenarios.corridor: US→EG High; TBML (OS-TM-008/030–032/035)
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened
scenarios.rating: High, EDD — and if the invoicing has no apparent lawful economic purpose → decline (§6.2)
scenarios.documents: Full trade documentation — commercial invoices, bills of lading, customs declarations, contracts — for reconciliation against declared flows; supplier/buyer KYB
scenarios.expectedActivity: Trade values reconciling to shipping/customs; genuine unrelated counterparties
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Over/under-invoicing; goods/values/routes don't add up; proceeds via unrelated intermediaries; circular trade
scenarios.sarPosture: No lawful economic purpose / TBML → SAR; decline
scenarios.outcome: Onboard High/EDD if documents reconcile; decline + SAR if goods/values/routes don't add up

scenarios.title: Egyptian holding — intercompany transfers with undeclared common control (round-trip)
scenarios.profile: EG holding moving 'intercompany' funds between entities whose common ownership was not declared; circular intra-group round-trips manufacturing turnover. Group/holding · multiple SPVs · common control partly undeclared.
scenarios.geography: Establishment EG · UBOs EG · SoW/SoF = group funds
scenarios.flow: B2B-09 intercompany transfer (same group); related-party test: group-relationship registry MANDATORY before the purpose is selectable; undeclared 'group' = hidden ownership
scenarios.corridor: US→EG High; circular-flow detection stays ON (TM 5.9)
scenarios.screening: All group nodes + UBOs screened; auditor-confirmed structure at EDD
scenarios.rating: High → decline on undeclared common control / round-trip
scenarios.documents: Group-relationship registry; auditor-confirmed group structure; intercompany agreements; netting analysis (gross vs net)
scenarios.expectedActivity: Declared, registry-backed intercompany treasury flows
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Undeclared 'group' transfers (hidden ownership); circular intra-group round-trips; gross ≫ net; turnover manufacturing
scenarios.outcome: Proceed for registry-declared groups with netting monitoring; decline + SAR on undeclared control / round-trip

scenarios.title: Turkish trading company — dual-use goods to a counterparty adjacent to sanctioned networks
scenarios.profile: TR company trading machinery/electronics (some dual-use) with a foreign counterparty; unusual routing/transshipment. LLC · clean single UBO resident TR · resolves to natural person.
scenarios.geography: Establishment TR · UBO TR · SoW/SoF = business income
scenarios.flow: B2B-02 goods (import/export) — dual-use; counterparty sanctions-adjacent; related-party test: counterparty & end-user screened, transshipment routing checked
scenarios.corridor: US→TR Medium — dual-use/sanctions-evasion adjacency; EDD mandatory
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO clear; COUNTERPARTY + end-use export-control screening is the crux; adverse media
scenarios.documents: KYB; end-user/end-use documentation; goods specifications; export-control screening of counterparties; shipping routes
scenarios.expectedActivity: Trade to screened, non-adjacent, licensed counterparties with clean end-use
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Dual-use to sanctions-adjacent parties; unusual routing/transshipment; end-user opacity
scenarios.sarPosture: Dual-use to sanctions-adjacent parties (PF/sanctions-evasion) → escalate/decline; SAR
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, High/EDD only on clean end-use + counterparty screening; decline/escalate on dual-use to sanctions-adjacent parties

scenarios.title: Bangladeshi garment SME — payroll-funding via a WPS/payroll agent (ghost-worker watch)
scenarios.profile: BD garment SME funding payroll through a licensed payroll/WPS agent; some supplier payments. LLC · UBOs resident BD · resolves to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment BD · UBOs BD · SoW/SoF = manufacturing income
scenarios.flow: B2B-08 payroll funding / WPS transfer to a licensed agent; B2B-01 supplier payment; related-party test: agent licensed & unrelated, wage-file linkage
scenarios.corridor: US→BD Medium — ghost workers, garment-TBML adjacency
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/agent screened
scenarios.documents: KYB; wage-file linkage; headcount plausibility; agent licence; supplier invoices
scenarios.expectedActivity: Payroll funding matching a registered wage file/headcount; supplier payments matching invoices
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Payroll funding >> declared headcount (ghost workers at scale); agent-misuse; supplier set implausible
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, Medium–High. Watch payroll-to-headcount divergence and agent-misuse → SAR

scenarios.title: Indonesian e-commerce company — supplier payments to a circular ring (transaction laundering)
scenarios.profile: ID online-retail company paying suppliers; supplier set implausible vs licence; circular B2B rings manufacturing turnover. LLC/partnership · UBOs resolve to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment ID · UBOs ID · SoW/SoF = marketplace revenue
scenarios.flow: B2B-01 goods / B2B-04 services — supplier counterparties; related-party / circular-ring check across suppliers
scenarios.corridor: US→ID Medium — circular supplier rings, transaction laundering, VA-linked B2B
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened
scenarios.documents: KYB; supplier KYB + plausibility vs licence activity (business-misuse scenario 5.22); invoice-pattern analytics
scenarios.expectedActivity: Supplier payments to a plausible, unrelated supplier set matching the business
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Supplier set implausible vs licence; circular B2B rings; round-amount invoice grids; turnover manufacturing
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, Medium with supplier-plausibility monitoring; decline + SAR on circular rings

scenarios.title: Filipino services company — commission/agency fees to a shell intermediary (kickback)
scenarios.profile: PH services company paying agency/commission fees to another business (broker/intermediary) with no underlying-transaction linkage. Corporation/LLC · UBOs resolve to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment PH · UBOs PH · SoW/SoF = business income
scenarios.flow: B2B-12 commission / agency fees — intermediary counterparty; related-party test: intermediary-web analysis, shell-intermediary check
scenarios.corridor: US→PH Medium — bribery/kickback conduit; corruption overlay
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened; PEP screening of the intermediary's principals
scenarios.rating: High → decline on shell-intermediary / no linkage
scenarios.documents: Agency agreement + underlying-transaction linkage; intermediary KYB; commission-to-revenue ratio
scenarios.expectedActivity: Commission tied to a documented underlying transaction to a genuine intermediary
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Agency fees to shell intermediaries; no underlying transaction; bribery/kickback conduit; PEP-linked principals
scenarios.outcome: EDD; decline + SAR on shell-intermediary / no underlying linkage

scenarios.title: Filipino gaming / POGO operator — declined at G2 (prohibited payer type)
scenarios.profile: A gaming/casino/POGO operator seeking to run B2B payments. Gaming/POGO — prohibited business type (§6.4).
scenarios.geography: Establishment PH
scenarios.flow: Would-be B2B payments to suppliers/agents; related-party test N/A — stops at G2
scenarios.corridor: US→PH Medium; prohibited-type stops before corridor scoring
scenarios.screening: Prohibited-type screen at G2 fires before any KYB
scenarios.documents: None — no document rescues a prohibited payer type
scenarios.expectedActivity: N/A
scenarios.behaviourWatch: N/A
scenarios.outcome: Decline at G2 (gaming/casinos prohibited)

scenarios.title: Saudi contracting JSC — construction/contracting payments (procurement-corruption overlay)
scenarios.profile: SA joint-stock company making progress payments to contractor businesses; some state-contract linkage; PEP-adjacent counterparties. JSC · layered shareholding resolved to natural-person UBOs; board resolution + shareholder list held.
scenarios.geography: Establishment SA · UBOs SA · SoW/SoF = business income
scenarios.flow: B2B-14 construction/contracting; B2B-12 agency fees — contractor/intermediary counterparties; related-party test: counterparty + principals screened, procurement red-flag overlay
scenarios.corridor: US→SA Medium — procurement corruption, state-contractor trade, commission-to-PEP
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO screened; PEP/state-linked screen of counterparties/principals; adverse media
scenarios.rating: Medium, escalating to High on PEP-linked / procurement red flags
scenarios.documents: KYB (incl. board resolution, shareholder list); contract + milestone evidence; counterparty KYB; PEP screening; procurement-corruption overlay
scenarios.expectedActivity: Progress payments matching contract milestones to genuine contractors
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Progress payments without project evidence; procurement-corruption patterns; PEP-linked counterparties
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, Medium; High + corroboration + senior sign-off on PEP-linkage; decline/SAR on procurement corruption

scenarios.title: Saudi holdco with a Yemeni UBO — restricted-country UBO (Rule 1), paying an unrelated business
scenarios.profile: SA-established holdco; one UBO is a Yemeni national with partial SoW from Yemen; pays an unrelated licensed business for services. Holding/LLC · UBOs = SA + Yemeni (restricted, non-OFAC) · resolves to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment SA (permitted) · UBO nationality YE · UBO SoW = partial Yemen · entity SoF = SA income
scenarios.flow: B2B-04/05 services — unrelated licensed counterparty; no Yemen counterparty/corridor; related-party test: counterparty unrelated & verified
scenarios.corridor: US→SA Medium + restricted-country-UBO screening; Yemen counterparty/corridor blocked
scenarios.screening: Yemeni UBO → screening escalation + Rule 1 on Yemen-origin SoW; entity/other nodes/counterparty clear
scenarios.rating: High floor (restricted-country UBO SoW, Rule 1) — documented UBO SoW, nexus screening, senior approval
scenarios.documents: Full KYB; Yemeni UBO SoW: documentary origin + how proceeds left Yemen (remittance receipts) + nexus screening + senior-approval record; service contract; confirm no Yemen counterparty
scenarios.expectedActivity: Service payments to unrelated verified counterparties; UBO capital evidenced
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any Yemen counterparty/corridor (→ reject); unexplained UBO top-ups from Yemen
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, High-floor EDD, senior sign-off; Yemen counterparties disabled. Unevidenced UBO SoW → hold then decline

scenarios.title: Jordanian company — CSR / charity donation to an NPO counterparty (whitelist + TF watch)
scenarios.profile: JO company making corporate charity/CSR donations to an NPO; some beneficiaries in conflict-adjacent regions. LLC · UBOs resident JO · resolves to natural persons.
scenarios.geography: Establishment JO · UBOs JO · SoW/SoF = business income
scenarios.flow: B2B-19 charity / CSR donation — NPO counterparty; related-party test: NPO licence + beneficiary/region screening
scenarios.corridor: US→JO Medium-High — conflict-adjacency TF/NPO, charity channels; enhanced EDD
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO screened; LICENSED-CHARITY WHITELIST hard control on the NPO; region/beneficiary TF screening
scenarios.rating: High / enhanced EDD; TF indicators → rapid reporting
scenarios.documents: KYB; NPO registration/licence + whitelist status; purpose; onward-flow/region screening
scenarios.expectedActivity: Donations to whitelisted licensed NPOs, proportionate; no conflict-adjacent onward flows
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Donations to unlicensed/foreign collectors; conflict-adjacent onward flows; NPO-diversion
scenarios.sarPosture: Unlicensed/conflict-adjacent NPO or TF indicators → decline + STR/SAR (rapid reporting)
scenarios.outcome: Proceed for whitelisted licensed NPOs; decline + STR for unlicensed/conflict-adjacent NPOs

scenarios.title: UAE trading FZE — re-export goods to an unrelated foreign buyer (workable, re-export TBML watch)
scenarios.profile: UAE free-zone trading company paying for re-export goods with genuine substance and an unrelated licensed buyer/supplier. FZE · clean UBO resident UAE · free-zone licence + establishment card · genuine substance.
scenarios.geography: Establishment AE (permitted) · UBO UAE-resident · SoW/SoF = trade income
scenarios.flow: B2B-02/03 goods (re-export) — unrelated licensed counterparty; related-party test: payer & counterparty unrelated & verified, no circular/transit round-trip
scenarios.corridor: UAE domestic Medium; register rates UAE a Critical re-export hub — watch onward chaining
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened; onward-destination check
scenarios.documents: KYB (free-zone licence, incorporation, establishment card, UBO pack, statements); trade docs (invoices, bills of lading, customs) for reconciliation; counterparty KYB; substance evidence
scenarios.expectedActivity: Re-export trade reconciling to docs, to unrelated verified counterparties, no onward-restricted nexus
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Re-export TBML; circular/transit trade; round-tripping; 3rd-party payers; onward to a restricted destination
scenarios.sarPosture: Mismatch or circular trade (TBML) → decline/exit + SAR
scenarios.outcome: Onboard, High/EDD if it reconciles; decline/exit on mismatch or circular trade (TBML)

scenarios.title: UAE free-zone shell — no substance, circular invoicing, multi-MSB routing (decline)
scenarios.profile: Free-zone entity with no real substance paying 'suppliers' with circular invoicing; same customer using multiple MSBs/wallets for one route. FZE · thin/no substance · UBO resolvable but no economic footprint.
scenarios.geography: Establishment AE · UBO varies
scenarios.flow: B2B-01/04 goods/services — circular counterparties; related-party test: circular-ring / no-external-counterparty check, related-party invoicing
scenarios.corridor: UAE domestic Medium; free-zone shell (typology 52)
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened; adverse-media/shell markers
scenarios.documents: Substance evidence (premises, utilities, employees); trade docs + genuine external counterparty; if no substance / circular invoicing → decline
scenarios.expectedActivity: Genuine operating B2B with substance and external counterparties
scenarios.behaviourWatch: No substance; circular invoicing; no genuine external counterparty; multi-MSB routing for one route
scenarios.sarPosture: No lawful economic purpose → decline/exit + SAR
scenarios.outcome: Decline/exit + SAR

scenarios.title: UAE LLC — dividend / capital injection to a corporate outside the ownership chain (round-trip)
scenarios.profile: UAE LLC paying 'dividends' to a corporate shareholder outside the declared ownership chain, or 'capital injection' into a shell (round-tripping). LLC · shareholder registry + UBO chain on file.
scenarios.geography: Establishment AE · UBOs UAE/mixed
scenarios.flow: B2B-10 dividend to corporate shareholder / B2B-18 capital injection; related-party test: UBO-CHAIN MATCH hard control (dividends), investee UBO refresh (capital), round-trip detection
scenarios.corridor: UAE domestic Medium; ownership-inconsistency (TM 5.23); round-tripping
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened; ownership-chain match
scenarios.rating: High → escalate/decline on chain mismatch / round-trip
scenarios.documents: Dividend: UBO-chain match + board resolution + profit plausibility. Capital: share documents + investee UBO refresh; round-trip detection
scenarios.expectedActivity: Dividends only within the declared ownership chain; capital into genuine, substantive investees
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Dividends to entities outside the chain (hidden ownership); 'investments' into shells; capital round-tripping
scenarios.sarPosture: Hidden ownership / round-tripping → SAR; UBO refresh
scenarios.outcome: Proceed for chain-matched flows; escalate/SAR + UBO refresh on mismatch / round-trip

scenarios.title: UAE entity — payment to a sanctioned / onward-Iran counterparty (block)
scenarios.profile: UAE entity attempting a B2B payment to a business that is sanctioned or an onward-to-Iran re-export conduit; dual-use goods context. Any legal type.
scenarios.geography: Establishment AE (permitted) BUT sanctioned/onward-Iran nexus on the counterparty side
scenarios.flow: Any B2B payment to a sanctioned / Iran-nexus business; related-party test: counterparty screening fails
scenarios.corridor: Iran comprehensively sanctioned — any nexus blocks (CRAM §7.1)
scenarios.screening: G1 fails — sanctioned counterparty node
scenarios.documents: None — no document cures a sanctioned nexus
scenarios.expectedActivity: N/A
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any sanctioned/onward-Iran counterparty on an existing customer → block
scenarios.sarPosture: Block + SAR; OFAC blocking report within 10 business days
scenarios.outcome: Block + SAR + OFAC report

scenarios.title: IP/licensing holdco (any corridor) — royalty/IP fees to a low-transparency jurisdiction (profit-shifting)
scenarios.profile: An IP-holding company paying royalty/IP fees to a related or low-transparency counterparty; pricing outliers. Holdco/LLC · UBO chain resolved.
scenarios.geography: Establishment = a permitted corridor · UBOs vary · counterparty in a low-transparency jurisdiction
scenarios.flow: B2B-13 royalties / IP fees; related-party / transfer-pricing check central
scenarios.corridor: Corridor-scored; transfer-pricing-style outlier tests; corridor risk
scenarios.screening: Payer/UBO/counterparty screened; secrecy-jurisdiction flag
scenarios.documents: Licence agreement; pricing basis; transfer-pricing outlier tests; counterparty KYB
scenarios.expectedActivity: IP fees at arm's-length pricing under a genuine licence to a substantive counterparty
scenarios.behaviourWatch: IP fees as a profit-shifting/mispricing channel to low-transparency jurisdictions; pricing outliers
scenarios.outcome: EDD; escalate/decline on mispricing to low-transparency jurisdictions

scenarios.title: Regulated FI / licensed VASP (end-user) — B2B settlement (permitted-with-EDD; nested/correspondent prohibited)
scenarios.profile: A genuinely licensed, directly-onboarded FI/VASP settling B2B flows; NOT a shell bank / nested relationship / domestic correspondent / 3rd-party processor. Regulated FI / licensed VASP · directly onboarded end-user · UBO chain resolved.
scenarios.geography: Establishment = a permitted corridor · UBO chain resolved
scenarios.flow: B2B settlement flows; related-party test: confirm NOT nested / correspondent / 3rd-party processor
scenarios.corridor: Corridor-scored; §6.3 FI taxonomy; §12 Priority-3 High floor pending EDD
scenarios.screening: Entity/UBO/counterparty screened; Travel-Rule capability; programme attestations
scenarios.documents: Wolfsberg DDQ; valid licence; AML/sanctions programme + attestations; Travel-Rule capability (data-survival tested). Prohibited: shell banks, nested relationships, domestic correspondents, 3rd-party processors
scenarios.expectedActivity: B2B settlement consistent with a licensed FI's operations
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Nested/correspondent structure; missing Travel-Rule; pass-through
scenarios.outcome: Onboard High/EDD on a clean programme; decline on weak controls / missing Travel-Rule / prohibited FI structure
